title: A retrieval device for removing an element from an eye and method of retrieval
abstract: A retrieval device for removing an element from an eye and a method of retrieval disclosed relates to the field of ophthalmology. The retrieval device and the method of retrieval are used to remove any element from within the eye. The device and the method do not make any complication to the structure of the eye. The retrieval device comprises a plurality of wires and a sheath. The plurality of wires is connected at an operative end thereof defining a blunt end. The sheath is configured to partially cover the plurality of wires. The sheath and the plurality of wires are movable relative to each other, thereby facilitating a formation of a retrieval basket at the blunt end. The size of the retrieval basket is varied by displacing the sheath over the plurality of wires as per the size of the element to be retrieved from the eye.
claims:
Claims A retrieval device for removing an element from an eye, said retrieval device comprising: a plurality of wires connected at an operative end thereof to define a blunt end; and a sheath defining a lumen and configured to partially cover said plurality of wires, said sheath and said plurality of wires being movable relative to each other, thereby facilitating a formation of a retrieval basket at said blunt end, wherein the size of said retrieval basket can be varied, in accordance with the size of the element to be retrieved from the eye, by displacing said sheath over said plurality of wires. The retrieval device as claimed in claim 1 , wherein each of said plurality of wires is made of a kink-resistant super elastic metallic alloy. The retrieval device as claimed in claim 2, wherein said kink-resistant super elastic metallic alloy is nickel titanium alloy. The retrieval device as claimed in claim 1, wherein the number of wires in said plurality of wires is four. The retrieval device as claimed in claim 1, wherein said plurality of wires has a cross sectional configuration selected from a group consisting of D-shape, B-shape, V-shape, and semi-circular shape. The retrieval device as claimed in claim 1, wherein at least two adjacent wires of said plurality of wires are interconnected by a wire mesh to securely capture the element to be retrieved from the eye. The retrieval device as claimed in claim 4, wherein said plurality of wires includes: a first set of wires and a second set of wires, wherein said first set and said second set of wires are defined by two wires which are spaced 180Â° apart with respect to each other, such that all the wires of said first set and said second set of wires are spaced 90Â° 

 apart from each other, wherein said first set of wires is adapted to move in tandem with said second set of wires to facilitate the opening and closing of said retrieval basket, thereby allowing the capture and retrieval of the element from the eye. 8. The retrieval device as claimed in claim 1, wherein said lumen is configured to receive a fragmatome equipment and/or an endo-illumination probe. 9. A method for retrieving an element from an eye, said method comprising the following steps: introducing the distal end of a retrieval device in the eye such that said distal end abuts the eye; displacing a plurality of wires relative to a sheath of said retrieval device to configure a retrieval basket at said distal end; capturing the element in said retrieval basket; and displacing said retrieval device along with the element captured in said retrieval basket away from the eye. 10. The method as claimed in claim 9, which further includes the following steps: inserting a fragmatome equipment inside a lumen of a sheath of said retrieval device; advancing said fragmatome equipment proximal to the element captured within said retrieval basket; and emulsifying, using said fragmatome equipment, the element captured within said retrieval basket.
